what_it_does: This noticeboard item frames the design context for a dragnet system, summarizing prior design conclusions and extending them into a concrete operator-facing mobile annotation capability.
observations: References Doc #1 in the channel as the prior design discussion.
The system is converging on one pattern: a typed observation attached to an addressed node, resolved against scale, scope, and attachment context.
Nodes are addresses, observations are marks/comments, and edges are typed relations, with a unified walkable graph as a next step.

An annotation/comment is defined as a typed observation enriched with a context envelope: target address(es), captured content, surrounding context, scale/level, mode, source/storage, attachments, intent, author, provenance, and thread.
Scope and access are proposed to live as edges/frames in the same graph rather than a separate system.
Open architectural forks include convergence of three channel systems, membership gating data access, and the need for a real document model of addressable nodes.
The concrete use case is Tim annotating both content and the UI from his phone, with envelopes routed to Claude-Code members who act and reply.
standouts: Unifies annotations, comments, and observations into a single typed-observation-on-node pattern.
Proposes that scope, access, and routing should be graph edges rather than separate subsystems.
Extends the abstract design map into a concrete mobile operator annotation scenario.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Body is Markdown with a top-level heading.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link to a parent item.
File is filed as current by a specific author session.
concerns: Several key terms (dragnet, the three channel systems, Claude-Code members) are used without definition inside this file.
The context envelope is listed as a set of fields but not given a concrete schema.
Open architectural forks are noted but not resolved or assigned ownership.
The relationship between this item and item-11da929d is only labeled part_of, with no explanation of how it extends the parent.
notes: A design-context brief that maps prior dragnet abstractions onto a mobile annotation scenario and flags unresolved architectural forks.
questions: What is the exact address/identifier scheme for a node?
What are the three channel systems that may converge?
What does Claude-Code members mean as a routing target?
What is the concrete schema for the context envelope?
How does this item extend versus merely summarize item-11da929d?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it provides context and extends the prior design map into a concrete operator-facing capability.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared conceptual substrate so that a mobile annotation feature can be built on a unified typed-observation graph, while surfacing unresolved architectural decisions.
novelty: It treats annotations, comments, scope, access, and routing as typed observations or edges in one walkable graph rather than as separate subsystems.
contribution: The typed-observation-on-addressed-node pattern and the context-envelope field checklist as a design substrate for annotations, routing, and access control.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design brief that situates a dragnet system inside a unified typed-observation graph and extends it toward a mobile annotation scenario.
intention_for_embedding: To align the team on a shared context model and expose unresolved architectural forks before building a phone-based annotation feature.
